titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert L. Calhoun Papers
titlestmt.author: Abigail Kromminga and Noah Duclos

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 25, 2024 at 23:00

did.abstract: This collection documents the professional life of Robert L. Calhoun (1896-1983), Yale professor of theology and philosophy, including extensive materials relating to his work advocating for Christian non-violence, labor rights, and ecumenism. The papers provide insight into Calhoun's teaching and his response to the pressing social issues of the twentieth century including union rights, conscientious objection, Marxism, atomic war, and emergent "World Order" in the postwar. The papers contain substantial materials from Calhoun's organizational work with the Council for Social Action and the World Council of Churches among others and reflect his prominent role in American and ecumenical Protestant Christianity in the twentieth century.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the family of Robert Lowry Calhoun, 2022.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert L. Calhoun Papers (RG 326), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Lowry Calhoun was born in St. Cloud, Minnesota in 1896. He received a BA from Carleton College in 1915 before moving East to Yale University for a BD and MA received in 1918 and 1919. Following a year of study at Lincoln College, Oxford in 1919-1920, Robert Calhoun received his doctorate from Yale University in 1923. After a brief interval teaching at Carleton College, he returned to Connecticut for the rest of his life. In 1924 he married Dr. Ella Wakeman Calhoun, a graduate of the Yale School of Medicine. They raised four children together and welcomed two further English refugee children during WWII. After moving from New Haven, the family lived on a farm in Bethany, Connecticut, where Ella worked as the town director of public health.

At Yale, Robert Calhoun divided his time teaching philosophy and theology between the Divinity School and Yale College. His comprehensive lecture courses "History of Philosophy" and "History of Doctrine" drew hundreds of students, some of whom carefully wrote down his words, circulating them privately for decades. Beyond Yale, he lectured across the United States at institutions like Harvard, the University of Chicago, and the Jewish Theological Seminary of America, and preached in chapels and churches throughout the Northeast.

His own theology was, in the words of his close friend and colleague Roland Bainton, "from the bottom up…beginning with where we are, and with what makes sense out of where we are…God is vastly above yet closer than hands and feet." His published works, among which are God and the Common Life (1935) and God and the Day's Work (1943), focused on the immediacy of God in contemporary society.

Robert Calhoun's organizational involvement reflected his concern with the violence and alienation of the modern world. During the Depression, he joined the Citizens Committee on Unemployment. During WWII, concerned with both violence and coercive state power, he headed the New Haven Anti-Nazi League and the New Haven Committee for Conscientious Objectors. In 1946, he led a Federal Council of Churches commission condemning the American bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki stating, "the moral cost was too high. We have sinned grievously against the laws of God and against the people of Japan." In the fifties, he relentlessly studied and addressed the emergent "world order" through ecumenical work, serving high-up in several World Council of Churches committees.

In 1965, after forty-three years of teaching, Robert Calhoun retired. He died in 1983 at the age of eighty-six. His lifetime saw the violence of two world wars, economic depression, and dramatic global change—realities he continually intellectually engaged. Yet in his opening address at the 1954 World Council of Churches Evanston Assembly, Robert Calhoun declared, "the Kingdom of God is of all present realities the most real."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Robert L. Calhoun taught philosophy and theology at Yale from 1923 until his retirement in 1965. His papers document nearly a half century of teaching and intellectual work as well as ecumenical and social activism from the 1930s to 1960s—pertaining to labor rights, conscientious objectors, pacifism, and atomic war. The papers provide an exceptional resource for the study of the Protestant ecumenical movement and Christian social activism during the Great Depression, WWII, and the Cold War.

The Robert L. Calhoun papers arrived at Divinity Special Collections in considerable disarray. The collection follows at times Robert Calhoun's multiple organizational schemas and other times an order decided upon and implemented by the archivist. For assistance navigating the collection, reach out to Yale Divinity Special Collections staff.

Series I, CORRESPONDENCE, is organized chronologically dating from 1922 until 1983. The series contains circulars from advocacy groups, organizational correspondence, invitations to speak at conferences and chapels, and personal correspondence with religious scholars in the United States and abroad, including with Virginia Corwin, Wilhelm Pauck, Henry Pit VanDusen, Edwin Aubrey, Rufus M. Jones, Cornelius Kruse, Emil Brunner, and Louis Finkelstein. Calhoun's network of correspondents reflects his place in an East Coast theological milieu which included notable theologians like his Theological Discussion Group colleagues Reinhold Niebuhr and Paul Tillich and like-minded Christian activists including A.J. Muste.

Series II, SERMONS, WRITINGS, NOTES, contains three main subseries. The first subseries "Sermons and talks" follows Robert Calhoun's organization. The folders are organized by creation date, and each sermon is paired with an envelope on which Calhoun marked the multiple locations and dates the sermon was preached. The subseries holds several lectures and talks organized with the sermons. The second subseries "Publications" contains chronologically organized articles and essays written or co-written by Robert Calhoun. The subseries includes Calhoun's lecture transcripts, which were bound and circulated privately—later organized into the book History of Christian Doctrine. The third subseries "Drafts, talks, and essays" holds material intended for eventual publication along with conference talks and paper presentations. The subseries begins with book drafts in folders labeled by Calhoun. The following materials, prepared for a broad range of events and purposes, are organized by subject.

Series III, TEACHING AND ADMINISTRATIVE ACTIVITIES, is organized into two main subseries: "Teaching" contains course syllabi, lecture transcripts, and student notes; "administration" contains programmatic materials from the philosophy and religion department as well as miscellaneous materials related to Yale University and Yale Divinity School. The teaching materials within series III provide a window into the Robert Calhoun's intellectual range—from the history of the early Christian church to modern philosophy—and the role he played in bridging between the Divinity School and the philosophy department.

Series IV, ORGANIZATIONAL INVOLVEMENT, contains two subseries: "Organizations" which proceeds alphabetically by organization, and "Events" which is organized chronologically. For materials relating to Calhoun's work with labor rights, see materials relating to the American Association of University Professors, the American Federation of Teachers, the Citizens Committee on Unemployment, the Connecticut Conference on Social and Labor Legislation, and the Religion and Labor Foundation. For materials relating to Christian social action, see the Council for Social Action. Further folders related to Calhoun's advocacy work, particularly during the 1940s are in Series V, subject files, subseries "social issues and contemporary society." For materials related to Robert Calhoun's ecumenical work, see the Universal Christian Council for Faith and Work and the World Council of Churches.

Series V, SUBJECT FILES, consists of folders retaining their initial labeling from Calhoun's various office and study cabinets, and substantial materials—such as papers, academic journals, and leaflets—added into the series based on their thematic relation. The folders are organized into three main series: "Social issues and contemporary society," pertaining to Calhoun's advocacy work, "Individuals," largely retaining Calhoun's own labeling and organization, and "Topical gathered materials," which bring together gathered material on similar subjects. Overall, Series VI provides insight into the sources Robert Calhoun gathered to inform both his social advocacy and academic work. The first main subseries, "Social issues and contemporary society," is divided into topical folders organized thematically and gathered articles organized chronologically and by publication. The folders thematically organized often retain Calhoun's initial labelling despite topical overlap, such as "Pacifism," "Peace," "Conscientious Objectors," and "Conscription." They contain correspondence related to Calhoun's advocacy work, such as materials in "Atomic energy and warfare" regarding the Federal Council of Churches statement against weapons of mass destruction and substantial materials from the Council of Social Action in the "Conscientious Objectors" folders, relating to Robert Calhoun's work as chairperson of the New Haven Committee for Conscientious Objectors. Notable within the topical files is the folder labeled "Marxism," containing materials from the 1934 symposium on Marxism and Religion, at which both Robert Calhoun and Reinhold Niebuhr spoke. The gathered materials organized chronologically and by publication provide insight into Calhoun's concern with global and American politics in an age of mass violence. The second main subseries, "Individuals," consists of folders mostly labeled by Robert Calhoun and is organized alphabetically. The folders often contain correspondence with the individual or their writings. The third main subseries, "Topical gathered materials," brings together materials initially loose within Calhoun's papers based on their topical relation.
Series VI, BIOGRAPHICAL DOCUMENTATION, contains miscellaneous personal ephemera, articles and papers written about Robert L. Calhoun, and a folder of photographs.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Some materials collected by Robert L. Calhoun relating to the Theological Discussion Group and World Council of Churches were separated out from the collection and added to RG 43 and RG 162 respectively. These materials related to organizational activities outside of Calhoun's direct purview.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including family correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to Ella Calhoun, letters regarding the death of Ed, Robert's brother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 14 letter addressed to "Religious Realists" from D.C. Macintosh

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including related to YMCA and the National Council on Religion in Higher Education. See also: Organizational involvement, Series IV. National Council on Religion in Higher Education, box 49.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters of sympathy regarding the death of Calhoun's mother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding American Workers Party symposium "Marxism and Religion" and from Reinhold Niebuhr, Virginia Corwin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: August 31 letter regarding the political and religious environment in Nazi Germany.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including correspondence to Elton Trueblood and November 22 letter about Paul Tillich.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 10 "An open letter to Christian ministers and Christian leaders who publicly advocate pacifism and internationalism," from Howard Taylor Oliver.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Henry Pitt VanDusen and from J.H. Oldham on 1937 Oxford Conference and Emil Brunner's paper "Die christliche Lehre vom Menschen." See also: Sermons, writings, notes, Series II. "Review of Emil Brunner "Die christliche Lehre vom Menschen," box 29, folder 16. And organizational involvement, Series IV. Universal Christian Council for Faith and Work, boxes 51-52.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May 30 letter from Henry P. VanDusen regarding the Oxford Conference on Church, State, and Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: July 7 letter to Henry P. VanDusen regarding Oxford Conference. Letters from Luther Weigle and Roland Bainton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from James Rowland Angell, Yale president.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding the Oxford Conference and the Theological Discussion Group.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Further Oxford correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inluding correspondence regarding Calhoun's writings. See also: Sermons, writings, notes, Series II. "The Dilemma of Humanitarian Modernism," box 27, folder 12.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including correspondence with Devere Allen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including materials from the Connecticut Society of Mental Hygiene.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inlcuding January 27 correspondence with Rabbi Simon Greenberg regarding the establishment of a Jewish state in Palestine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inlcuding a February 17 letter to the Jewish Theological Seminary of America containing Calhoun's draft "A Christian Looks at Judaism."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 17 letter from Charles Reynolds Brown, former dean of the Divinity School, disagreeing with a sermon Calhoun preached on the war.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1942 includes correspondence relating to Calhoun's chairing of the Conscientious Objector Committee for New Haven.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials from the years 1943-1945 contain substantial correspondence relating to conscientious objectors, including from the ACLU and the Congregational Christian Committee on Conscientious Objectors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: March 27 letter from Denny Wilcher, in jail for conscientious objection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May 2 letter from Robert Calhoun describing his views on military intervention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1946 includes hate mail addressed to Calhoun due to his condemnation of the American bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials organized by Robert Calhoun. The dates on which the talks were delivered are written on the envelopes in each folder.

did.unittitle: "Communion talks," "An informed talk on the Bible," "Amiable Idlers and Servants of God," "Hosea's Principle of Reconstruction"
"False Balances of Love," "Kingdom of God is like unto leaven," "Plain People and the Word of God"
"Stagnation or Growth," "Ye must be born again," "The preacher and his message"
"The Person of Jesus," "Wooden Idols," "Christmas; Advent; Way of Pride and Way of Devotion"
"The Inevitable Christ," "Gospel in a time of Depression," "Good Friday"
"Church's Sphere of Action," "Church + the Spirit of God," "Gospel of Life + Death"
"Youth + the Chuch," "What Think Ye of Christ," "Church Militant"
"War and the Will of God," "Pacifist as Patriot," "Christian Realism + Task of Rebuilding/Days Ahead"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also containing "Religion's Stake in Peace," "Kingdom of God as Truth," "Kingdom of God as Beauty."

did.unittitle: "The Kingdom of God as Justice, Love," "Dream of American Independence"
"Religion + Light," "Foundations of Faith," "War + the Peace of God"
"Democracy + Modern Temper," "We are God's Fellow Workers," "Bull Worship + the Unseen God"
"Some Ancestors to Modern Schools of Religion," "Religion, Science, and Life," "The Right to Self Realization"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also containing "Augustine: a great theologian," and "The Relation of the Church School to the Church."

did.unittitle: "Can I Believe in the Bible and Evolution?" The Adult Bible Class Magazine
"Can God and Evolution go Together?" The Adult Bible Class Magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Theology in Strenuous Days," "Religion: Prose or Poetry?," "Moral Man and Immoral Society," Review of Normative Psychology of Religion .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Religious Realism edited by Douglas Clyde Macintosh.

did.unittitle: "Christian Conscience and the State," Social Action

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jointly authored with Roland Bainton.

did.unittitle: "The Role of Historical Theology," The Journal of Religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In Social Action issue "The Struggle for World Order," by Vera Micheles Dean.

did.unittitle: "Thought and Action in Religious Living," The Journal of Religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Plain Citizens," 1942?, "Kierkegaard's Writings," 1945, "Two New Volumes of Kierkegaard," 1947.

did.unittitle: "The Relation of the Church To the War in the Light Of the Christian Faith," Social Action

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jointly authored by the Federal Council of the Churches of Christ in America executive committee.

did.unittitle: "The Place of Religion in Higher Education"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Print of the Hazen lectures delivered at the Bicentennial Conference of the University of Pennsylvania.

did.unittitle: "The Christian Conscience and Weapons of Mass Destruction"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A jointly-authored report by the special commission appointed by the Federal Council of the Churches of Christ in America. For related materials, including drafts of the statement and Calhoun's research see also: Organizational involvement, Series IV. Federal Council of Churches, Atomic warfare commission, box 47, folders 10-12. And Subject files, Series V. "Atomic energy and warfare," box 63, folders 1-4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Addresses from the North American Lay Conference of the Christian and His Daily Work in Buffalo, New York, February 1952.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "First Report of the Advisory Commission on the Theme of the Second Assembly of the World Council of Churches," 1951? "Professor Calhoun Reports on Evanston, 1954," Yale Divinity News . See also: Organizational involvement, Series IV. World Council of Churches.

did.unittitle: "Christ the Hope of the World," Motive Magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: World Council of Churches issue on Evanston 1954. For further files on the WCC Evanston assembly, see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. World Council of Churches, Evanston Assembly 1954, box 54-56.

did.unittitle: "Christ The Hope of the World," The Ecumenical Review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For further materials on the World Council of Churches Faith and Order conference in Oberlin in 1957, see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. World Council of Churches, Faith and Order, Oberlin Conference 1957, box 59, folders 3-9.

did.unittitle: "Reason and Revelation in Religious and Knowledge"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a printed booklet of Robert Calhoun's Dudleian Lecture at Harvard University as well as notes, drafts, and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "The Redemption of Time" and "Ad Interim."

did.unittitle: "Democracy and Natural Law," Natural Law Forum
"Paul Schubert at Yale," in Studies in Luke-Acts: Essays in Honor of Paul Schubert
"A Christian Comments on The Problem of Dialogue Between Judaism and Christianity," Education in Judaism

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including drafts and notes.

did.unittitle: "A New History of Christian Doctrine A Review Article," Journal of the American Academy of Religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Calhoun's bound lectures were circulated by former students and later reformatted into Scripture, creed, theology: lectures on the history of Christian doctrine in the first centuries edited by George A. Lindbeck.

Lectures on the History of Christian Doctrine Volume III
Lectures on the History of Christian Doctrine
Correspondence regarding God and the Common Life
Correspondence regarding God and the Common Life
Correspondence regarding Democracy and Divine Law
Typescript bound draft God and the Common Life
Democracy and Natural Law final draft and notes
Democracy and Natural Law draft and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Freedom and Responsibility: An Inquiry Concerning the Metaphysical Foundations of Ethics"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "A Discussion of Human Freedom" and "The Problem of Evil as Related to a Christian God."

did.unittitle: YDS paper on the problem of evil
YDS papers "Royce's Doctrine of the Atonement" and "The Outlook for the Rural Church"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Epistemology," "History of Ethics," and "Teaching of Jesus."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Old Testament Theology" and "New Testament."

did.unittitle: Oxford class notes and miscellaneous class notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Philosophy of Religion" and "Social Psychology."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "A Statement of Faith," n.d. and "How My Mind Has Changed in this Decade," 1939.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including account of early contact with Dean Luther Weigle and "On the Shooting at Yale Divinity School - A Christian's View."

did.unittitle: Advocacy and commentary on contemporary society

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper written for the 1937 Life and Work Conference of the Universal Christian Council for Life and Work in Oxford. Including further drafts and comments by colleagues. Due to poor health, Calhoun did not attend the conference. See also: Organizational involvement, Series III. Universal Christian Council for Faith and Work, boxes 51-52. And correspondence, Series I. 1936-1937.

did.unittitle: Temple of Religion at the World's Fair address and "Religion in Defense of Humanity"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address given at the World's Fair Temple of Religion in New York, July 31, 1939, printed in The Jewish Forum , September 1939.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Is God Pacifist?" 1943?, "The Theological Basis For Pacifism: a discussion held in the Y.D.S. Common Room," with Calhoun and H. Richard Niehbuhr, 1950, and "A Statement on Compulsory Military Training in Peacetime," International Relations Committee, C.S.A. For further materials relating to the Council for Social Action see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. Council for Social Action, box 45-47.

did.unittitle: "The Importance of the Bill of Rights Today"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For further materials on the Connecticut Conference on Social and Labor Legislation, see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. Connecticut Conference on Social and Labor Legislation, box 44, folders 8-9.

did.unittitle: "The Relation of the Church To the War in the Light Of the Christian Faith"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For the Federal Council of Churches of Christ in America. For further materials regarding Calhoun's work on atomic warfare see also: "The Christian Conscience and Weapons of Mass Destruction," box 23, folder 19. And Organizational involvement, Series III. Federal Council of Churches, Atomic warfare commission, box 47, folders 10-12. And Subject files, Series V. "Atomic energy and warfare," box 63, folders 1-4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For further materials on the Corning Conference see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. Events, Corning Conference, "Living in Industrial Society," box 62, folders 1-6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For the Yale University Christian Mission conference, February 3-6, 1952.

did.unittitle: "Actions of Congregational Christian Churches Concerning International Relations Between Two Wars" and "Confidence in Life in Industrial Civilization"
Drafts on world order, technology, and civilization

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "God as More than Mind," 1935 and "How Far Beyond Humanism," 1938.

did.unittitle: Review of Emil Brunner "Die christliche Lehre vom Menschen"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper written for the 1937 Life and Work Conference of the Universal Christian Council for Life and Work in Oxford. For further materials related to the Universal Christian Council for Life and Work, see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. Universal Christian Council for Faith and Work, boxes 51-52.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rauschenbach Lectures at Colgate-Rochester Divinity School. Including "Church, State, and Human Devotion."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and gathered materials relating to the lectures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For further materials related to the Theological Discussion Group, see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. Theological Discussion Group, box 51, folders 2-6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including drafts for the Universal Christian Council for Life and Work.

did.unittitle: "Time, Eternity, and the End of History" and "The Holy Spirit in the Trinity"
"Notes on Hope in the Bible and in our Report"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: WCC paper, related to the Oberlin Conference. For related materials see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. World Council of Churches, Faith and Order, Oberlin Conference 1957, box 59, folders 3-9.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a review of Paul Tillich "Protestantism as Criticism and Creation."

scopecontent.p: Including "Mental Hygiene and the Minister's Theology."

"Christian Faith and Daily Work" and "The Day's Work as Christian Vocation"

"The Meaning of the Humanities" and "Sciences and Humanities in Higher Education"

scopecontent.p: Speech at the Federeation General Committee meeting of the WSCF. For related materials, see: Organizational involvement, Series IV. World Student Christian Federation, box 61, folders 2-3.

"Contemporary Intellectual Climate and Mood of the Campus" and "Confronting University Life and Values with the Christian Faith"
"Religion and the Intellectual Life" and "The Quest for Religious Certainty"

scopecontent.p: Containing the draft "The Semi-detached Knower: A Note on Radical Empiricism," potentially for The Nature of Religious Experience , 1937.

did.unittitle: "Reason and Revelation in Religious and Knowledge"
"Notes on the Consultation on Intellectual Freedom"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing correspondence and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Calhoun's poetry.

did.unittitle: "Intro to Philosophy," "Philosophy of Life," "Ancient and Medieval Philosophy"
"Lectures on the History of Philosophy Class Notes"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume composed by student Carl Nelson of Calhoun's lectures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including notes for the lecture, "Schleiermacher Religion as Feeling."

did.unittitle: Lecture transcripts: ancient, Kepler, Copernicus, Galileo, Smith
Lecture transcripts: Descartes, Pascal, Hobbes, Spinoza, Leibniz, Locke, Berkeley
Lecture transcripts: Hume, Kant, Fichte, Schelling, Hegel, Schleiermacher, Kierkegaard, Comte, Mill, McTaggart, Spencer, Marx

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Calhoun's gathered notes for the course, including his own student lecture notes from YDS and notes on The Varieties of Religious Experience , and lecture plans.

did.unittitle: Lecture transcripts: early Judaic, heresy and orthodoxy, Arianism
Lecture transcripts: Anselm, Peter Abelard, Peter Lombard
Lecture transcripts: high scholastics, Aquinas, Duns Scotus, William of Ockham, John Wyclif
Lecture transcripts: Luther, Calvin, radicals, Trent, Westminster, Augsburg

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding courses taught at Harvard Divinity School and Union Theological Seminary.

did.unittitle: History of Doctrine, Doctrine of Salvation, Religion and Religious Life in the United States
Sermons by William Sloan Coffin Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Admission and Program for Returning Service Men."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Committee on the Admission of Women report and "Women at Yale Divinity School" study.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing the "Christian Social Teachings" seminar series in 1936 on questions of sex, politics, economics, and pacifism, and the 1944 series "The Contemporary World Crisis."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "Winning the Peace" and "A Statement and a Demand."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Calhoun's memorial of D.C. Macintosh, 1948, Benjamin E Mays "The Christian in Race Relations," 1952, and materials regarding the RSV translation.

did.unittitle: American Academy of Political and Social Science

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the brass workers' union in Waterbury, Connecticut.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the tenure dispute between the institution and Albert S. Keston.

did.unittitle: New York University notes, writings, and official documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Free Thought and Free Thinking: The Bertrand Russell Case," 1940 and "No Charter for Totalitarianism," 1939? on the relation to the Communist Party post Hitler-Stalin pact.

did.unittitle: Related to the Working Conference for Peace
Correspondence, circulars, papers to be presented
Correspondence, circulars, papers to be presented
Correspondence, circulars, papers to be presented

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials on the effect of the depression on public health.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlets on labor organizing, including "Working class unity bulwark agains fascism," and from the labor party in the United States. Including 1935-1936 Social Trends from the Connecticut Emergency Relief Commission.

did.unittitle: Connecticut Conference on Social and Labor Legislation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Calhoun's drafts of circulars regarding World War II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "A Declaration on Post-War Reconstruction," April 18, 1942.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Granada Relocation Project: A Visit to Study the Problem of Relocation," 1944 on Japanese-American deportation and "Post-War Aims" and "Post-War Needs," 1945.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including materials on relief, world order, conscription, and atomic energy.

did.unittitle: Study packet "War Issues Confronting the Churches"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to the Congregational Study for World Organization and the CSA International Relations Committee.

did.unittitle: Related to the "National Study Conference on the Churches and a Just and Durable Peace"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: World Order Conference held in Delaware, Ohio March 3-5, 1942. Containing correspondence, collected materials, and writings.

did.unittitle: Related to the "National Study Conference on the Churches and a Just and Durable Peace"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: World Order Conference held Cleveland, Ohio January 16-19, 1945.

did.unittitle: Writings and notes regarding world organization

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing writings by Calhoun and others including "Religious Proposals for World Order" by Liston Pope, 1941.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gathered materials on world organization containing circulars and pamphlets.

did.unittitle: Study file "World Order: Dumbarton Oaks, Yalta, San Francisco"
Study file "World Order: Dumbarton Oaks, Yalta, San Francisco"
Study packet "American Churches and World Order"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Toward a Better U.S. Foreign Policy."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials gathered for research in drafting the statement.

did.unittitle: Study booklet "Christian Action for a World in Crisis"
Divisional Program in the Humanities Princeton
Federal Council of Churches of Christ in America
Department of International Justice and Goodwill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing correspondence, papers, and pamphlets. Including "The Churches and the European Recovery Program" and "A Positive Program for Peace."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence related to the Dun Commission. See also: Sermons, writings, notes, Series II. "The Christian Conscience and Weapons of Mass Destruction," box 23, folder 19. And "Atomic Warfare and the Christian Faith," box 28, folder 6. And Organizational involvement, Series III. Federal Council of Churches, Atomic warfare commission, box 47, folders 10-12. And Subject files, Series V. "Atomic energy and warfare," box 63, folders 1-4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "The Churches and the Hydrogen Bomb" and the commission report "The Christian Conscience and Weapons of Mass Destruction." Including Calhoun's brief statement on why he witheld his signature from the final report.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding Robert Calhoun's 1952-1953 Fulbright exchange year in the Netherlands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known as the National Committee on Food for the Small Democracies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including November 30, 1940 letter from Herbert Hoover.

did.unittitle: Gathered papers on the Hoover Plan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Can Christians Refuse to Feed the Hungry?" by Eve Garrette.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the Institute on Ethics of the Institute for Religious and Social Studies at the Jewish Theological Seminary of America. Including correspondence from Louis Finkelstein. For further correspondence between Finkelstein and Calhoun, see: Correspondence, Series I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Reinhold Niehbuhr, "The Christian Faith and the Economic Life of Liberal Society."

Conference on Churches and World Order "The Christian Faith and Collective Security"
National Council on Religion in Higher Education

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Calhoun's notes in preparation for and on the conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For related writings by Robert Calhoun, see: Sermons, writings, notes, Series II. "Related to Hazen Conference," box 30, folder 9.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including the pamphlet "The Races of Mankind" by Ruth Benedict and Gene Weltfish, 1943.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New Haven-based labor rights organization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing correspondence, minutes, notes, and flyers. Including from the Workers Party of the United States.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Time, Eternity, and the End of History" by Robert Calhoun.

scopecontent.p: Including the 1930s series "What strategy should the Church adopt with reference to…" "Communism" by Paul Tillich, "Fascism" by Cornelius Kruse, and "Democracy" by Georgia Harkness.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "The Totalitarian Claims of the Modern State and the Christian Conception of Freedom," 1934, writer unknown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circulating paper by Robert Calhoun.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related materials in: Sermons, writings, notes, Series II. "The Dilemma of Humanitarian Modernism," box 27, folder 12. And "Review of Emil Brunner "Die christliche Lehre vom Menschen,"" box 29, folder 16.

scopecontent.p: Including by Emil Brunner and Paul Tillich.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding the conference at Woudschoten and the Oxford Conference.

scopecontent.p: Including "The Responsibility of the Church for the International Order," "Is the Church the Church in Wartime?," and "The Ethical Reality and Function of the Church" by W.A. Visser 't Hooft.

scopecontent.p: Including "Notes on the Situation of the Church in Europe" and materials from the nascent World Council of Churches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "The Ethical Reality and Function of the Church" by Emil Brunner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The study packet includes Christianity and Crisis "Notes on the European Situation" by W.A. Visser 't Hooft and reports on the World Council of Churches.

scopecontent.p: Including "Notes on the Attitudes of Chrsitians to this War" by W.A. Visser t' Hooft, 1939 and "Die englisch-amerikanischen Kirchen und die Friedensfrage" by Gerhard Ritter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "The World Council of Churches: Its Nature Its Limits" by W.A. Visser t' Hooft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Calhoun's notes on "Technics and Civilization" by J.H. Oldham.

scopecontent.p: Including Bulletin No.3.

scopecontent.p: Including materials relating to the meeting in Rolle, July 20-30, 1951 and "The Hope of the Church and the Hope of the World" by Karl Barth.

scopecontent.p: Containing drafts, circulars, and notes.

scopecontent.p: Including "The Advisory Commission at Bossey" and "Notes on Hope in the Bible and in our Report" by Robert Calhoun.

scopecontent.p: For further writings by Robert Calhoun on work and Christian vocation, see: Sermons, writings, notes, Series II. Drafts, Christianity and work, box 30, folders 4-6.

scopecontent.p: Including Ecumenical Studies introducing the assembly topics.

scopecontent.p: The bulk of the correspondence is related to Calhoun's activity as the chairperson for the North American Theological Commission for Christ and the Church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Travel Diary" series of reports from churches throughout the world.

scopecontent.p: Including reports from prior Faith and Order conferences and a booklet listing the delegates to Lund.

scopecontent.p: Publication series in advance of the 1957 Oberlin conference.

And "Atomic Warfare and the Christian Faith," box 28, folder 6. Organizational involvement, Series IV. Federal Council of Churches, Atomic warfare commission, box 47, folders 10-12. And Subject files, Series V. "Atomic energy and warfare," box 63, folders 1-4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a report of the roundtable discussion with Robert Calhoun, Cornelius Kruse, and Margaret Mead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Calhoun's notes and a draft of his writing "Confidence in Life in Industrial Civilization."

did.unittitle: Tenth Annual Institute of Higher Education
Conference on Science and Human Responsibility, Washington University
Conference on Science and Human Responsibility, Washington University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a binder with conference speeches.

did.unittitle: Campus Conference on Religion, Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gathered materials organized by subject matter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files arranged alphabetically, retaining Calhoun's original labeling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including the papers "Modern Warfare and the Christian Conscience" and "Hiroshima and the Future." See also: Sermons, writings, notes, Series II. "The Christian Conscience and Weapons of Mass Destruction," box 23, folder 19. And "Atomic Warfare and the Christian Faith," box 28, folder 6. And Organizational involvement, Series IV. Federal Council of Churches, Atomic warfare commission, box 47, folders 10-12.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "The relation of the Church To the War in Light of the Christian Faith," Social Action , 1944, and Federal Council of Churches of Christ statements on the atomic bomb.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "The Atomic Bomb and the American Dream" by A.J. Muste.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the Congregational conscientious objectors committee, a part of the CSA.

did.unittitle: Regarding the CPS unit in Middletown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, circulars, and clippings regarding the controversy at the Middletown State Hospital regarding the CPS unit #81 involving Calhoun's son David.

did.unittitle: "The Draft and You by Congressman Emanuel Celler"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials relating to the Fellowship of Reconciliation and the American Friends Service Committee. Including "Statement against the wartime adoption of peacetime conscription" by Divinity School students and professors and Calhoun's own notes and writings on the topic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing materials on Marxism and its relation to Christianity, including related to the 1934 symposium "Religion and Marxism, a conflict?" chaired by A.J. Muste, at which Robert Calhoun and Reinhold Niebuhr spoke. Including Calhoun's written remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from the General Conference Commission on World Peace and the Committee on Militarism in Education.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding pacifism and anti-pacifism.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "A Statement on Control of Armaments and Compulsory Military Training" and "Christian Action on Four Fronts for Peacetime."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "How shall we deal with a defeated Japan?"

did.unittitle: "The Church and Military Measures for Peace: A Rejoinder" by Roland Bainton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "American-Russian Relations: Some Constructive Considerations" from the American Friends Service Committee and "Contemporary Role of Russia and Stalinist Communism" by A.J. Muste.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to the National Advisory Work Camp Committee Board of Home Missions Congregational Christian Churches and the Committee for Social Action. Related to CPS work camps for conscientious objectors and students.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gathered materials arranged loosely chronologically and topically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to World War II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of the clippings are regarding the Kirchenkampf.

did.unittitle: On the transition to the postwar and peace

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Study Pamphlet on the War the Tansition Process and the Peace" and State Department bulletins including "Committee of European Economic Co-Operation General Report."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "A Christian Thinks About the War" by Herman F. Reissig, 1942 and "The Zionist Issue in Christian Theology" by Alan R. Taylor, 1964.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by periodical.

did.unittitle: National Council for Prevention of War News Bulletin and The Arbitrator
The Christian Newsletter and Christianity and Crisis
"The United States in a New World," Time Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including drafts of letters to the editor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gathered materials organized alphabetically by creator lastname.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing correspondence and articles about Buckley's scholarship on Yale history.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing God and Man at Yale by William F. Buckley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "The World Federation Plan: A System to Win this War and Win the Peace to Come."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the "religious realism" platform and group. For related material, see correspondence from 1929.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing the thesis "Proofs of the Fact of Original Sin."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ella Wakeman Calhoun's brother.

Basis of Union Evangelical Reformed and Congregational Churches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Featuring Roland Bainton describing his Quaker beliefs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Calhoun's notes on H.R. Niebuhr The Kingdom of God in America .

"Mershon Thesis: God of Judges and Modern German God"

scopecontent.p: Including by Roland Bainton on Luther.

Letters written by the philosopher Thomas Davidson
"Some Types of Nineteenth Century Theology" H.R. Mackintosh

scopecontent.p: Lectures on Schleiermacher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including by Edward Calhoun.

did.unittitle: Materials from Clark L. Hull's seminars

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Robert Calhoun attended a series of informal seminars held by Clark L. Hull, Yale professor of psychology. Folders relating to the seminars contain correspondence and lesson overviews.

did.unittitle: Materials from Clark L. Hull's seminars
Correspondence and notes regarding ordering records
Papers on sex, gender, and healthcare
Dr. Ella Calhoun, YWCA and mental hygiene
"The self survey general plan, Hugh Hartshorne"
Articles and pamphlets about Robert L. Calhoun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing articles on Calhoun, draft deferral, and conscientious objectors.

did.unittitle: "The Theology of Robert Lowry Calhoun" by Fred B. Eutsler
"The Devil's Advocate and Mr. Calhoun: a play in one act" by Theodore Sager Meth
"Robert Lowry Calhoun as a Historian of Doctrine" by George A. Lindbeck
